title: In Syracuse budget battle, council leader pledges to override all mayoral vetoes
summary: Syracuse Common Council Acting President Pat Hogan pledged to override all mayoral vetoes on a budget that cuts $16 million from Mayor Ben Walsh's proposal to address a projected $27 million deficit. Walsh indicated he will issue vetoes on Monday, but has no legal recourse once the council's nine members vote unanimously to override; department heads warn the cuts will eliminate jobs and threaten core services like fire response and parks programming.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

political_signal.description: Escalating public dispute between Mayor Walsh and Council leadership (Hogan) over budget priorities; council has locked in position with no compromise, mayor facing certain veto override with no legal remedies remaining.
